Requirement: All capacitors connected to a power net must have voltage ratings that are at least 20% higher than the rail voltage.
Explainability: Multiple capacitors (C1, C2, C3, C4, C6, C9) are connected to externally-supplied power nets (V_Batt, Input_KL30, VIN_LM5060) where the actual rail voltages cannot be determined from the schematic, preventing verification of the required 20% voltage rating margin.

⚠ C1 has a voltage rating of 80V and is connected to the V_Batt power net, which is supplied from an external source through connector J2. The actual voltage of V_Batt cannot be determined from the schematic (it could range from 3V to 60V based on downstream components). Therefore, it cannot be verified whether the 80V rating provides the required 20% margin above the rail voltage.

⚠ C2 is connected to power net V_Batt, which is supplied from an external source via connector J2. The exact voltage of V_Batt cannot be determined from the circuit design alone, so the 20% voltage rating margin requirement cannot be verified.

⚠ C3 is connected to Input_KL30, which is an externally supplied automotive battery input. The exact rail voltage cannot be determined from the circuit design alone as it depends on the external battery voltage, which can vary (typically 9-16V nominal, with transients up to 24V based on the TVS diode clamping voltage). C3 has a 100V voltage rating.

⚠ C4 is a 100V rated capacitor connected to the Input_KL30 power rail (through C3). However, Input_KL30 is supplied from an external source through connector J1, so the actual rail voltage cannot be determined from the schematic. Cannot verify if the 100V rating meets the 20% margin requirement without knowing the rail voltage.

✓ C5 is connected to power net VIN_LM5060, which has a maximum voltage of 60V (clamped by TVS diode D8 SMAJ60A).
✓ C5 has a voltage rating of 80V, which exceeds the required minimum of 72V (60V × 1.20 = 72V with 20% margin).

⚠ C6 has a voltage rating of 100V, but the VIN_LM5060 rail voltage cannot be determined because it is supplied from an external connector (J5) with no specified voltage in the schematic or documentation. Without knowing the rail voltage, the 20% margin requirement cannot be verified.
✓ C7 is connected to NetC7_1 (TIMER pin of U1) and GND. NetC7_1 is not a power net, so the voltage rating requirement for capacitors on power nets does not apply to C7.

✓ C8 is connected to the VCC_5V power rail, which is a 5V rail generated by U3 (TPS7A1650DGNR fixed 5V LDO regulator).
✓ C8 has a voltage rating of 25V, which exceeds the minimum required rating of 6V (5V × 1.20 = 6V).

⚠ C9 has a voltage rating of 100V and is connected to the V_Batt power rail, which is supplied from an external source through connector J2. The actual operating voltage of V_Batt cannot be determined from the schematic alone, as it depends on the external battery source connected. While the board is designed for automotive applications (typically 12V nominal, ranging 9-24V based on the LM5060 application example), and C9's 100V rating would exceed the 20% margin for typical automotive voltages, the rule cannot be definitively verified without knowing the actual V_Batt voltage specification.

✓ C10 is connected to the VCC_5V power net, which is a 5V rail generated by the TPS7A1650 regulator (U3).
✓ C10 has a voltage rating of 25V, which is at least 20% higher than the 5V rail voltage (minimum required: 6V).

✓ C11 is connected to the VCC_5V power rail, which is a 5V rail generated by U3 (TPS7A1650DGNR LDO regulator).
✓ C11 has a voltage rating of 10V, which is at least 20% higher than the 5V rail voltage (required minimum: 6V).

Explainability: Failed because internal power rails V_Internal and VCC_5V lack required test points or test point interfaces.

✓ VIN_LM5060 has test point TP2 connected to it, satisfying the requirement even though it's exempt.

✓ V_Internal is not directly connected to an external connector (it connects to Input_KL30 through protection and switching components).
✗ V_Internal has no test point or test point interface connected to it. The only components on this net are D1, D2, D7 (diodes) and Q1, Q2, Q3 (transistors).

✓ VCC_5V is an internal power rail generated by U3 (TPS7A1650DGNR LDO regulator) and is not connected to any external source or load.
✗ VCC_5V does not have a test point or test point interface connected to it. The board has test points TP1, TP2, TP3, and TP4, but none are connected to VCC_5V.

Explainability: All checks passed.

✓ The output voltage of U3 (TPS7A1650DGNR) is 5V, which matches the voltage requirement of U2 (LM2903AVQD) that accepts 2V to 36V supply voltage.
✓ The maximum output current of U3 is 100 mA, which is greater than the typical current draw of U2 (0.8-1 mA).

Requirement: All components with reset, enable, and logic pins must have these pins connected such that they are never left floating. These pins must either be connected directly to a power net or have a pull-up or pull-down resistor.
Explainability: The EN pin is connected through Q4, but the rule could not be fully verified because additional details about Q4's configuration are needed to confirm whether this connection prevents floating conditions under all operating states.

⚠ The EN pin is connected through Q4, but additional details about Q4's configuration are needed to verify if this constitutes a proper connection that meets the design rule requirement for control/logic pins to be 'connected directly to power nets or through pull-up/pull-down resistors'.

✓ U3 has no reset pin. The datasheet confirms the only control input requiring a defined logic level is the EN pin.
✓ U3's PG (Power Good) pin is an output pin connected to GND, which is acceptable per the datasheet.

⚠ OVP (pin 3) on NetR5_1 has voltage of 0.0286 × Input_KL30 via R5/R8 divider (510kΩ/15kΩ). Required threshold: 2.0V (1.88-2.12V range), Absolute max: 75V. Cannot verify - requires Input_KL30 voltage value.

✓ Input_KL30 operates from 5.5V to 65V, which is pulled up to the PGD net through R10 (10kΩ).
✓ When PGD is high-impedance (no fault condition), it is pulled up to Input_KL30 voltage (5.5V to 65V).
✓ A voltage divider is formed by R13 (261kΩ) from PGD to NetR13_2, and R15 (18kΩ) from NetR13_2 to GND. R16 (10kΩ) from NetR13_2 to Disable also affects the calculation.
⚠ The worst-case voltage on NetR13_2 (pin 3 of U2) needs to be calculated considering the maximum Input_KL30 voltage of 65V. The parallel combination of R15 (18kΩ) and R16 (10kΩ) to ground is approximately 6.43kΩ. Using the voltage divider formula: V_NetR13_2 = V_PGD × (R_parallel / (R13 + R_parallel)) = 65V × (6.43kΩ / (261kΩ + 6.43kΩ)) ≈ 1.56V.
⚠ The calculated worst-case voltage on NetR13_2 is approximately 1.56V when Input_KL30 is at its maximum of 65V. This needs to be verified against the maximum input voltage rating of U2 pin 3.

✓ The datasheet confirms that the non-inverting input (+) can exceed VCC level, while the inverting input (-) has an upper common-mode limit of VCC-1.5V.
✓ Both inputs can withstand up to 30V without damage according to the datasheet.
⚠ Need to verify which pin of U2 is the non-inverting input (+) and which is the inverting input (-) to complete the evaluation.
